message:
test(readme): complete Series Expanding + Ewm coverage (fd90.283 / br-4iare)

Extends readme_window_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover 9 more
window methods, completing the Series-level window surface:

Series Expanding (6 added — completes the 14-method surface):
- count, quantile(0.5), kurt, skew
- corr(&other), cov(&other) (window stats against same-length Series)

Series Ewm (3 added — completes the 6-method surface):
- sum, corr(&other), cov(&other)

With this commit, all Series-level windows (Rolling 18 + Expanding
14 + Ewm 6 + Resample 13 = 51 method-invocations) are exercised.

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-4fp","title":"Add timedelta_total_seconds conformance test coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T22:26:22.048342487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T22:30:14.675011684Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T22:30:14.673053056Z","close_reason":"Added SeriesTimedeltaTotalSeconds operation with full conformance harness support. Created 3 test fixtures. Also fixed parse_hms bug where -00:01:00 was not recognized as negative.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-4h1dm","title":"fd90.261: Expose date_range/timedelta_range/DateOffset (pandas-parity)","description":"fd90.261: Expose date_range / timedelta_range to top-level + prelude (pandas-parity).

These are pandas-equivalent of pd.date_range() and pd.timedelta_range() — common index-construction helpers. They're at fp-index lines 1821 and 2272 (date_range) but not exposed in frankenpandas at all.

Also expose:
- DateOffset (line 2011) — used by date_range
- DateRangeError (line 1869) — error variant
- TimedeltaRangeError (line 1793) — error variant
- apply_date_offset / apply_date_offset_to_nanos
- infer_freq + variants

Scope:
- Add date_range, timedelta_range, DateOffset, DateRangeError, TimedeltaRangeError, apply_date_offset, apply_date_offset_to_nanos, infer_freq to top-level fp_index re-export
- Add date_range, timedelta_range, DateOffset to prelude (most user-facing)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.

Top-level: date_range, timedelta_range, DateOffset (with Day/Hour/etc. variants — caught the variant naming during compile guard write: it's Day(1), not Days(1)), DateRangeError, TimedeltaRangeError, apply_date_offset, apply_date_offset_to_nanos, infer_freq. Prelude: date_range, timedelta_range, DateOffset (most user-facing). All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-4hkt","title":"fp-conformance: live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling min|max|count","description":"Add mock-free fp-conformance coverage against the live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling(window).min(), max(), and count(). Use interleaved groups plus missing values so windows prove group-local scoping and null-count semantics. Capture parity through phase2c artifacts instead of fp-frame-only unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-22T05:29:31.198884127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:22:53.966730221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T07:22:53.964890760Z","close_reason":"shipped 3269824: 2 fp-frame tests (groupby_rolling_min_max_count_int64_input_widens_to_float, groupby_rolling_window_one_emits_per_row_identity) complementary to x5oy's Float64 window=2 baseline. Locks Int64->Float64 dtype widening on rolling aggregations + window=1 degenerate identity invariant. Clippy clean; 8/8 groupby_rolling tests green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-4iare","title":"fd90.283: Cover remaining Expanding methods + Ewm.corr/cov","description":"fd90.283: Cover remaining Expanding methods + Ewm.cov + Series Ewm corr.

Series Expanding has 14 methods; fd90.199 covered 8 (sum/mean/min/max/std/var/median/apply). Missing 6: count, quantile, kurt, skew, corr, cov.

Plus Series Ewm has 3 covered (mean/std/var) but cov + corr if available.

Scope:
- Extend readme_window_operations_compiles_and_runs.

Zero new tests; existing tests still cover both code paths via the public API entry points.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"refactor","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:01:51.545666940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:04:21.087401826Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:04:21.084956386Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","refactor","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-4m90b","title":"fd90.183: README Quick Start + case_when use new Scalar From .into() ergonomics","description":"fd90.74-182 follow-on. fd90.182 added From<bool/i64/f64/&str/String> impls for Scalar. Update the README's Quick Start example to use the new ergonomic .into() pattern so readers see the cleaner syntax instead of the explicit Scalar::Utf8(...) form.

Before (line 222):
  let dates = Series::from_values("d", vec![0i64.into()], vec![Scalar::Utf8("2024-01-15".into())])?;

After (cleaner via fd90.182):
  let dates = Series::from_values("d", vec![0i64.into()], vec!["2024-01-15".into()])?;

Also updates the case_when example (lines 1086-1090) where Vec<Scalar> values are constructed via repeat for "Good"/"Poor"/"Excellent" — the inner Scalar::Utf8(...) wrappers can become "Good".into() etc.

Pure README change — fd90.182 already shipped the From impls.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:08:36.244959583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:12:33.417764029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:12:33.415681515Z","close_reason":"Updated README Quick Start (line 222) and case_when example (lines 1086-1090) to use the new From<&str>/From<i64>/etc ergonomics introduced by fd90.182. Mirrored the same change in tests/readme_quick_example.rs:60. All 22 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
